video:video.video:title: US Congress approves temporary funding for Homeland Security
video:video.video:description: The US Senate passed a funding deal early Friday, but blowback from House Republicans came quickly. House Speaker Mike Johnson, upon opening the chamber for business, accused Democrats of playing a dangerous game and said he needed to talk with fellow Republicans about how to proceed.

video:video.video:title: Once a luxury for unconventional moms, doula care is going mainstream
video:video.video:description: Doulas provide support and guidance before, during and after birth. Once a luxury, now doulas are becoming mainstream, with insurance coverage growing and parents from across the economic spectrum using them.

video:video.video:title: Inside astronaut training for NASA’s moon mission
video:video.video:description: This is how astronauts trained for NASA’s Artemis II mission, which will send humans back to the moon for the first time since Apollo 17 in 1972.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia starts implementing social media restrictions for children under 16
video:video.video:description: Indonesia on Saturday began implementing a new government regulation approved earlier this month that bans children younger than 16 from access to digital platforms that could expose them to pornography, cyberbullying, online scams and addiction.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy meets UAE’s Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed during Gulf tour
video:video.video:description: Zelenskyy meets UAE’s Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed during Gulf tour

video:video.video:title: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels claim responsibility for missile attack on Israel
video:video.video:description: The Iranian-backed Houthi rebels have claimed responsibility for a missile attack on Israel, their first since the war in the Mideast started. Brig. Gen.

video:video.video:title: At least one killed in Tel Aviv following the Iranian attack towards Israel on Friday night
video:video.video:description: At least one person was killed in Tel Aviv following the Iranian attack towards Israel on Friday night.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rising in Tehran following airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Several explosions were heard in various areas across Tehran on Saturday. Iran's semi-official Fars news agency said at least four locations in Tehran were targeted on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Centuries-old craft of Easter egg decorating takes center stage each spring at festival in Leipzig
video:video.video:description: In a city better known for Bach and book fairs, a quieter tradition takes center stage each spring: the centuries-old craft of Easter egg decorating.

video:video.video:title: USS Massachusetts joins the Navy fleet amid ongoing war with Iran
video:video.video:description: The USS Massachusetts has joined the Navy fleet, making it the first submarine named after the Bay State. (AP Video: Michael Casey, Robert Bukaty)

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon kills 3 journalists covering the war
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon has killed three journalists who were covering the Israel-Hezbollah war, according to their TV stations.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon kills 3 journalists covering the war - AP explains
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed three journalists who were covering the latest Israel-Hezbollah war, their TV stations said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 28
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, March 28th: Houthis enter the conflict in the Middle East; Protestors hold "No Kings" rallies against Trump; US Congress approves temporary funding for Homeland Security; Authorities release Tiger Woods on bail after DUI arrest.

video:video.video:title: Nepal police arrest former PM and former home minister over September protest deaths
video:video.video:description: Police in Nepal arrested former Prime Minister Khadga Prasad Oli early Saturday over the deaths of dozens of people during violent protests in September that toppled the government and resulted in new elections.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV in Monaco to urge its people to use their faith and wealth for good
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV urged residents of the cosmopolitan Mediterranean principality of Monaco on Saturday to use their wealth, influence and Catholic faith for good, especially to uphold Catholic teaching on protecting the sanctity of life.

video:video.video:title: Protests in Nepal after former PM and home minister arrested over protest deaths
video:video.video:description: Protesters took to the streets of the Nepali capital Kathmandu on Saturday after police arrested former Prime Minister Khadga Prasad Oli over the deaths of dozens of people during violent protests in September that toppled the government and resulted in new elections.

video:video.video:title: Protesters gather in Paris for 'No Kings' rally
video:video.video:description: Several hundred people, mostly Americans living in France, joined for the first time by French labor unions and human rights organizations, gathered Saturday morning at the Bastille in Paris for a "No Kings" rally.

video:video.video:title: French police foil suspected bomb plot outside Paris Bank of America
video:video.video:description: French authorities say that police have stopped a suspected bombing outside a Bank of America building in Paris.

video:video.video:title: 11 injured in missile attack in central Israel
video:video.video:description: Israel’s rescue service Magen David Adom said 11 people were wounded in an Iranian missile attack in Eshtaol, near Jerusalem.

video:video.video:title: 'It was an amazing moment' - faithful share their joy as Pope Leo XIV visits Monaco
video:video.video:description: Faithful shared their joy on Saturday as Pope Leo XIV visited Monaco. Leo made a one-day trip to the glitzy enclave, becoming the first pope to visit since Pope Paul III came in 1538.

video:video.video:title: Funerals held in Khan Younis, Gaza for Palestinians including 3 police killed in Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Two Israeli strikes early Sunday killed six Palestinians, including three policemen, in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said.

video:video.video:title: 'No Kings' rallies draw crowds across US against Trump Adminstration
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people have joined “No Kings” protests across the U.S. against the war in Iran and President Donald Trump’s actions.

video:video.video:title: Drone falls in the heart of Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Drone falls in the heart of Baghdad

video:video.video:title: Humpback whale freed by rescuers in Baltic Sea has become stranded again
video:video.video:description: A humpback whale that was freed after becoming stuck for several days in shallow water at a Baltic Sea resort in Germany was stranded again on Saturday after failing to find its way back to the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Severe flooding hits Russia’s Dagestan region
video:video.video:description: Severe flooding hit Russia’s southern Republic of Dagestan on Saturday following heavy rainfall. A railway bridge collapsed, and power was cut off in 132 settlements across Dagestan.

video:video.video:title: A behind-the-scenes look at astronauts training for the Artemis moon mission
video:video.video:description: NASA is going back to the moon more than a half-century after Apollo. The Artemis mission with four astronauts on board won't land on the moon this time or even orbit it.

video:video.video:title: Inside the lab where NASA’s moon mission spacesuits are made
video:video.video:description: NASA is getting ready to send astronauts on a 10-day trip around the moon. The spacesuits they’ll wear are designed for the Orion capsule and future missions to Mars.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of airstrike on car service company in Tehran
video:video.video:description: An automotive services company in Tehran said it has suffered extensive damaged following an airstrike on March 1.

video:video.video:title: Inside the lab where NASA’s moon mission spacesuits are made
video:video.video:description: NASA astronauts are gearing up to fly around the moon for the first time in more than a half-century.

video:video.video:title: Iran-backed Houthis enter the monthlong war and could further threaten global shipping - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels entered the month-long war in the Middle East on Saturday, claiming two missile launches at Israel.

video:video.video:title: Jerusalem heads into a subdued Passover and Easter under the shadow of the Iran war
video:video.video:description: Jerusalem's major holy sites are shuttered and families are dejected and exhausted ahead of Passover and Easter as the Iran war rages on, now in its fifth week.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke rises from industrial site in southern Israel hit by fragment of intercepted missile
video:video.video:description: Source: ISRAELI FIRE AND RESCUE AUTHORITY Heavy black smoke was seen rising Sunday at an industrial plant in southern Israel after what Israeli authorities said was an "interception fragment impact” from an Iranian missile

video:video.video:title: Missing sailboats carrying aid land in Cuba after being located by Mexican navy
video:video.video:description: Two sailboats that went missing carrying humanitarian aid from southern Mexico to Cuba landed in Havana on Saturday afternoon hours after Mexico's navy said it had located the boats days after they went incommunicado because of bad weather.

video:video.video:title: AP reports: Iranians at border crossing into Iraq urge US to end the war
video:video.video:description: Iranians crossing into southern Iraq on Sunday urged the United States to end the war, describing relentless airstrikes, rising prices and worsening living conditions, but insisting they would not leave their country.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather at Chile harvest festival, celebrating wine and tradition
video:video.video:description: Thousands of visitors attended the Panquehue Harvest Festival in Chile’s Valparaíso Region on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Iran University of Science and Technology building reduced to rubble by Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: The country's Revolutionary Guard warned that Iran would consider Israeli universities and branches of U.S. universities in the region “legitimate targets” unless offered safety assurances for Iranian universities, state media reported.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 29
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, March 29: Pakistan's foreign minister says they will soon host talks betwen the U.S. and Iran; PM Netanyahu says Israel will widen its invasion of southern Lebanon; severe weather in Afghanistan kills at least 17 people; Jerusalem holy sites closed ahead of Passover and

video:video.video:title: Faithful celebrate Palm Sunday in Ecuador and Peru
video:video.video:description: Faithful in Ecuador and Peru celebrate Palm Sunday, marking the official start of Holy Week. (AP video shot by: Mauricio Muñoz)

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese on streets of Beirut condemn 'expansionist' Israel as it seeks to widen invasion
video:video.video:description: People in Lebanon displaced by the ongoing fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group condemned Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu's announcement he was widening Israel's invasion of the south of the country.

video:video.video:title: Egypt introduces 9 p.m. closing time for commercial premises in bid to save electricity
video:video.video:description: As Egypt seeks ways to conserve oil-powered electricity, one decision appears to hit at the core identity of Cairo as a city that prides itself on never sleeping.

video:video.video:title: Bird sellers carry towers of caged songbirds in annual Palm Sunday pilgrimage to receive blessings
video:video.video:description: For 32 years, Cruz Monroy has wandered the streets of a small town on the fringes of Mexico's capital with a tower of small cages filled with a rainbow of birds from all over the world.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian Catholics attend Palm Sunday Mass in Gaza City
video:video.video:description: Christians gathered at the Holy Family Church in Gaza to celebrate Palm Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian inmates reduce sentences through reading
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of Brazil's detainees are part of a programme where reading books reduces their sentences. (AP/ Lucas Dumphreys)

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo prays for Christians in the Middle East at the Vatican's Palm Sunday Mass
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Sunday rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St.

video:video.video:title: Pakistani foreign minister says his country will host talks between US and Iran
video:video.video:description: "Pakistan will be honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides in the coming days,” Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar said in a speech after top diplomats from Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia met in Islamabad.

video:video.video:title: Actor Antonio Banderas leads Palm Sunday procession in his hometown of Malaga
video:video.video:description: Actor Antonio Banderas joined the crowds in a Palm Sunday procession on Sunday, marking the official start of Holy Week in Spain, a country with a fervent Catholic tradition.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday night said he has “no problem” with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

video:video.video:title: Chesney the kangaroo scales tall fence and flees petting zoo in Wisconsin for three days
video:video.video:description: Chesney the kangaroo jumped an eight-foot fence to escape his petting zoo in Wisconsin, spending three days on the lam.
